elements.sent-2411-017: Black hole multipole moments play a crucial role in describing the external fields and gravitational wave radiation, potentially offering a new window into the footprints of quantum gravity \cite{Bena:2020see, Bianchi:2020bxa, Bianchi:2020miz, Bah:2021jno beyond standard General Relativity.}
elements.sent-2411-018: Upcoming observations of gravitational waves from extreme mass-ratio inspirals (EMRIs) \cite{LISA:2017pwj,Barack:2006pq,Cardoso:2016ryw,Babak:2017tow,Ryan:1995wh,Krishnendu:2018nqa may reveal whether black hole mass and spin multipole moments match predictions from classical general relativity or suggest modifications induced by new physics beyond the standard model.}
elements.sent-2411-019: It is thus urgent to understand in an effective theory of gravity, whether black hole multipole moments are meaningful physical observables, i.e. invariant under field redefinitions.
elements.sent-2411-020: Based on traditional approach, to obtain black hole multipole moments, one needs to first solve for higher derivative corrections to rotating black holes which is technically quite difficult.
elements.sent-2411-021: Until now, there is no proof that in a generic theory of gravity with higher derivative corrections, the black hole multipole moments are invariant under field redefinitions, except for very few specific examples.
elements.sent-2411-022: For instance, the recent work \cite{Cano:2022wwo has computed the multipole moments of Kerr black holes in pure gravity with cubic curvature corrections and showed that they are invariant under field redefinitions of the metric utilizing the Ricci flatness of Kerr solution.}
elements.sent-2411-023: For non Ricci flat solutions, such as Kerr-Newman black holes, the situation is unknown.
elements.sent-2411-024: In fact, it was speculated in \cite{Cano:2022wwo that for non-Ricci-flat solutions, higher derivative corrections to multipole moments might be affected by field redefinitions.}
elements.sent-2411-025: In this work, we make the first attempt to study leading higher derivative corrections to Kerr-Newman black hole, with focus on its multipole moments.

elements.sent-2411-041: This paper is organized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-042: In section 2, we study the electrically-charged Kerr-Newman black hole and obtain the mass and current multipole moments from the metric and the electric and magnetic multipole moments from the Maxwell field.
elements.sent-2411-043: In section 3, we consider the (leading) 4-derivative corrections, which can be categorized as parity even and parity odd terms.
elements.sent-2411-044: The leading order correction is solved order by order in terms of appropriate two dimensionless parameters $(\chi_a,\chi_Q)$ of the Kerr-Newman black hole.
elements.sent-2411-045: This allows us to read off the 4-derivative corrections to the multipole moments.
elements.sent-2411-046: We then show that the results are independent of the field redefinition, indicating that they are indeed good physical quantities in our effective theory approach to quantum gravity.
elements.sent-2411-047: We conclude the paper in section 4.
elements.sent-2411-048: In appendix A, we show how the corrections of the solution modify the thermodynamic variables, and the results are consistent with the Reall-Santos method.
elements.sent-2411-049: In appendices B and C, we give the complicated expressions that would be a digression if given in the main text.
elements.sent-2411-050: In appendix D, we discuss briefly the Geroch-Hansen method of multipole moments for general theories of gravity.

elements.sent-2411-123: Black hole multipole moments are potentially useful observables for probing the nonlinear structures of General Relativity and its modifications due to unknown UV physics.
elements.sent-2411-124: In this work, we made progress in addressing a key question: do higher derivative perturbative corrections to black hole multipole moments are invariant under field redefinitions that are not supposed to affect physical quantities in a low energy effective theory of quantum gravity.
elements.sent-2411-125: So far, traditional methods for calculating multipole moments have not explicitly demonstrated this property for general effective theory of gravity.
elements.sent-2411-126: As far as we are aware, the field redefinition invariance of black hole multipole moments has only been shown for $D=4$ Einstein gravity extended by cubic curvature terms \cite{Cano:2022wwo , where the Ricci flatness of the leading order solution had played a role in the proof.}
elements.sent-2411-127: Here, we further investigated the leading higher-derivative corrections (\ref{even ,\ref{odd ) to the non-Ricci flat Kerr-Newman black hole \eqref{Kerr-Newman .}}}
elements.sent-2411-128: We find that the leading higher derivative corrections to black hole multipole moments indeed depend only on the combinations of coupling constants that are inert under field redefinitions.
elements.sent-2411-129: To achieve this, we first cast the electrically-charged Kerr-Newman black hole in the ACMC coordinates.
elements.sent-2411-130: We read off the nonvanishing mass and current multipole moments \(\{M_{2n , \mathcal{S _{2n+1}\}\) from the metric, and the nonvanishing electric and magnetic multipole moments \(\{\mathcal{Q _{2n , \mathcal{P _{2n+1}\}\) from the Maxwell field.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-131: The absence of the magnetic monopole has a consequence that all the even magnetic multiple moments vanish, and the odd ones are generated by the electric charge under rotation.
elements.sent-2411-132: We then adopted an approximate method of solving for the higher derivative corrected metric and $U(1)$ gauge field order by order in dimensionless rotation parameter $\chi_a={a /{\m}$ and charge parameter $\chi_Q={q /{\m}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-133: To simplify the computation, we arrange the infinite series expansion in the basis of homogeneous polynomials of $\chi$'s.
elements.sent-2411-134: In $D=4$, the leading higher derivative corrections to Einstein-Maxwell theory are classified by their properties under parity transformations.
elements.sent-2411-135: For parity preserving 4-derivative interactions, we are able to obtain the perturbed solution up to ${\cal O (\chi^7)$.}
elements.sent-2411-136: Similar to the pure gravity case, 4-derivative interactions only add corrections to multipole moments that are actually nonzero at the leading order, namely, \(\{M_{2n+2 , \mathcal{S _{2n+3 , \mathcal{Q _{2n+2 , \mathcal{P _{2n+1}\}\) for $n\ge 0$.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-137: Concerning the parity odd 4-derivative interactions, we obtain the perturbed solution up to ${\cal O (\chi^8)$.}
elements.sent-2411-138: We find that while these higher-derivative terms do not contribute to black hole thermodynamics, they affect the perturbative solutions, thereby modifying the black hole multipole moments.
elements.sent-2411-139: Notably, they contribute to multipole moments \(\{M_{2n+1 , \mathcal{S _{2n , \mathcal{Q _{2n-1 , \mathcal{P _{2n}\}\) for $n\ge1$ that all vanish at the leading order.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-140: This feature has significant observational implications.
elements.sent-2411-141: If they are observed, it would indicate the presence of parity odd higher-derivative corrections to General Relativity.
elements.sent-2411-142: As for future directions, we would like to push further to consider the next to next leading order higher derivative corrections and check if black hole multipole moments remain invariant under field redefinitions.
elements.sent-2411-143: The current method only allows us to carry out a case by case verification, after solving for the perturbed solution.
elements.sent-2411-144: For more general theories of gravity, in order to prove field redefinition invariance of black hole multipole moments, one may need to resort to a new formalism such as the one based on covariant phase space approach \cite{Compere:2017wrj .}
elements.sent-2411-145: Inspired by previous work \cite{Mayerson:2022ekj , we propose generalizations of Geroch-Hansen formulae to broader gravity models, in which the generalized twist 1-form is derived using the covariant phase space approach.}
elements.sent-2411-146: The results are presented in Appendix \ref{GH .}
elements.sent-2411-147: At this moment, we have not succeeded in applying these results to prove the field redefinition invariance of multipole moments and would like to pursue this problem in future study.

elements.sent-2411-179: Utilizing the timelike Killing vector $\xi=\partial_t$, they constructed two scalars which encode the information about gravitational multipole moments.
